target: ISS-677: Mode 5 Specification for Analyst Prompt

specification.priority: LOW — after Modes 0-4 and normal analysis. Runs as lightweight check (~30 seconds) when no other mode has work.

dispatcher_check.bash: node -e "const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('data/predictions.json','utf8'));const p=d.entries.filter(e=>e.entry_type==='prospective');const active=p.filter(e=>e.dome_promotion_status==='active');const changed=p.filter(e=>e._status_changed);console.log(active.length?'PROS TRACKING: '+active.length+' active items':'NO ACTIVE PROS');console.log('Total PROS:',p.length,'Promoted:',p.filter(e=>e.dome_promotion_status==='promoted').length,'Suspended:',p.filter(e=>e.dome_promotion_status==='suspended').length)"
dispatcher_check.trigger: Always runs as a lightweight check. Primary trigger: poller detects PROS status change (new item, promotion, suspension, removal).

steps.name: Read cached dome predictions page
steps.detail: Parse raw-text/03-predictions.txt for current PROS items. Extract: PROS ID, status (active/promoted/suspended/removed), claim text, test conditions, and dome confidence.

steps.name: Compare against predictions.json
steps.detail: For each PROS entry in predictions.json with entry_type 'prospective': compare dome_promotion_status against dome page status. Flag changes.

steps.name: Handle status changes
steps.detail: If NEW PROS item: write pre-emptive critique to monitor/analyst/expansions/PROS-assessment-PROS-NNN.json with fields: dome_claim, standard_physics_explanation, testability, kernel_of_truth, predicted_verdict_if_promoted. If STATUS CHANGE (promoted/suspended/removed): write status-change report to monitor/analyst/expansions/PROS-status-YYYY-MM-DD.json. If promoted to WIN: cross-check that the WIN already exists in wins.json; if not, flag for Mode 0. If suspended/removed: propose FAIL entry to expansions/ for decider integration into uncounted-failures.json.

steps.name: Rate limit
steps.detail: Cap at 3 new critiques per run (same as Mode 1b). Status changes are unlimited since they're lightweight.

pre_emptive_critique.standard_physics_explanation: 2-3 sentences on how standard physics explains the same observation
pre_emptive_critique.kernel_of_truth: What the dome gets right about this observation

status_change_report.implications: string — what this means for the review

prompt_addition.location: Between Mode 4 (Globe Fingerprint Hunt) and Normal mode in analyst.md dispatcher
prompt_addition.rationale: Mode 5 is low priority but should run before the normal dome-change analysis because PROS status changes are a specific, structured check that takes <30 seconds. It does not consume the entire run — continue to Normal mode after Mode 5 completes.

current_state.note: All 8 PROS entries already have verdicts in predictions.json. Section 6.11 renders the prospective tracker analysis. Mode 5 is needed for ONGOING monitoring — detecting future PROS additions, promotions, and suspensions. Currently all items are terminal (promoted or suspended), so Mode 5 would be a no-op until the dome adds new PROS items.

other_side.argument: With 0 active PROS items and the dome in a quiet period, Mode 5 will run for months producing nothing. Adding complexity to the analyst prompt for a no-op mode is technical debt.
other_side.counter: The cost is 3 lines of bash check + a reference to a procedure file. The insurance value (catching silent PROS-003-style suspensions) justifies the minimal overhead. When the dome eventually adds new PROS items — likely around the next version update — Mode 5 will be ready.
